title: Funeral Director

rationales.taskAutomation: Coordinating services, handling remains, and supporting grieving families is licensed, high-trust, in-person work; only permit paperwork and scheduling are cleanly automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generic scheduling and document-generation tools apply, but purpose-built funeral-industry AI tooling has little traction.
rationales.adoption: The funeral industry is conservative and highly regulated; AI adoption is rare and confined to back-office administration.
rationales.agenticExposure: Death-certificate paperwork, permit filing, and scheduling are agent-ownable; family support and ceremonial judgment are not.

tasksGrowing: Grief support and family counseling during arrangements
Ceremonial judgment and personalization of services
Handling sensitive or complex family situations
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-4031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Grief support and family counseling skills
Licensing and continuing education in mortuary science
Digital paperwork and permit-filing tool fluency

insight: The paperwork around death, permits, certificates, scheduling, is genuinely automatable and slowly is being automated. Nothing about supporting a grieving family through a funeral is, and that's the part of the job that was never really about paperwork anyway.
